howItWorks.intro: This reverb time (rt60) calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: A = Total Absorption (sabins) = Surface Area × Average Absorption Coefficient
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is RT60?
faq.answer: RT60 is the time it takes for sound to decay by 60 dB (become inaudible) after the source stops. A large cathedral might have RT60 of 5+ seconds; a recording studio aims for 0.3-0.5 seconds.

faq.question: What RT60 is ideal for a home studio?
faq.answer: For music recording: 0.3-0.5 seconds. For speech/podcasting: 0.2-0.4 seconds. For mixing/mastering: 0.3-0.6 seconds. Living rooms typically measure 0.4-0.8 seconds.

faq.question: How do I reduce reverberation?
faq.answer: Add absorption: acoustic panels, thick curtains, carpet, upholstered furniture, bookshelves. Each material has a specific absorption coefficient at different frequencies. Treat first reflection points first.

faq.question: Is the Reverb Time (RT60) Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Reverb Time (RT60) Calculator?
sections.body: The Reverb Time (RT60) Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your room length, room width, room height, you get instant results including rt60, room volume, total absorption. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting rt60 right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your room length and need to find the right rt60. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
